---
title: "Hello, Python"
date: "2026-04-17"
format:
  gfm: default
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Click here to see Python code"
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Getting Help
```{python}
help(round) 
```

help() displays 2 things:
1. the header of the function name (here: round(number[, ndigits])), this means that we need to provide a number to round, and optionally give a seperate argument which could be described as ndigits.
2. a brief English description of what the function does.

# Defining functions and Docstrings

```{python}
def least_difference(a,b,c):
    """
    This part is Docstrings, which is used to help the programmer to under stand the function and give an example.

    Return the smallest difference between any two numbers among a, b and c.

    >>> least_difference(1, 5, -5)
    4
    """
    diff1 = abs(a-b)
    diff2 = abs(b-c)
    diff3 = abs(a-c)
    return min(diff1, diff2, diff3)
    # return is necessary, this means what the function will give back to you when you call this function

print(least_difference(1, 10, 100))
help(least_difference)
# Here help() will return the Docstrings
```

# Default Arguments

```{python}
print(1, 2, 3, sep=' < ')
# Here if we do not give the value'<', the default value will be used.
# And the default value is " "(a single space)
print(1, 2, 3)

def greet(who="Colin"):
    print("Hello,", who)
greet()
greet(who="Kaggle")
# (In this case, we don't need to specify the name of the argument, because it's unambiguous.) 写出参数名字是显示，隐式会根据顺序自动匹配
greet("world")          
```

# Functions Applied to Functions

```{python}
def mult_by_five(x):
    return 5 * x
def squared_call(fn, arg):
    return fn(fn(arg))
print(squared_call(mult_by_five, 2))
```
